message:
feat(repair): add multi-host repair ownership protocol and design

Implement lease-based repair ownership for multi-host coordination
(bd-m5wf.5.1). Adds CoordinationRecord with TTL-based expiry,
deterministic UUID tiebreak for conflict resolution, atomic
write-via-rename persistence, and RepairOwnership API
(try_acquire/renew/release/is_owned_by_us) with OwnershipGuard.

Also includes:
- Design document (docs/design-multi-host-repair.md) covering ownership
  model, conflict detection, symbol exchange wire format, and failure
  mode analysis (host crash, network partition, stale ownership,
  split-brain, clock skew)
- RAID6 parity wraparound regression test in ffs-btrfs
- Re-export new btrfs types (BtrfsDevItem, BtrfsRaidProfile,
  BtrfsStripeMapping, chunk_type_flags, map_logical_to_stripes,
  parse_dev_item) from ffs-ondisk
- Close issue bd-m5wf.5.1 in beads tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","title":"Implement stale-window SLO instrumentation and alerting","description":"## Purpose

Track stale-window percentiles (time and block-count since last refresh per group) and alert when SLO is exceeded. This bead focuses on the SLO *policy* layer (thresholds, breach detection, alerting); raw repair metrics (scrub rate, decode success) are handled by bd-m5wf.8.4. Uses the metrics framework from bd-m5wf.8.1 for counter/gauge/histogram primitives.

## Scope

1. **Stale-window metrics** — Per-group tracking of:
   - Time since last symbol refresh (uses Gauge from bd-m5wf.8.1)
   - Blocks written since last refresh (uses Counter from bd-m5wf.8.1)
   - p50/p95/p99 across all groups (uses Histogram from bd-m5wf.8.1)

2. **SLO definition** — Configurable SLO: max acceptable stale window (default: p95 < 60s OR p95 < 5000 blocks). SLO configuration via RepairConfig struct.

3. **Alert emission** — Structured log marker `repair_stale_window_slo_breach` when any group exceeds SLO. Does NOT duplicate the raw metric emission from bd-m5wf.8.4; only emits policy-level breach events.

4. **Evidence integration** — `ffs evidence --preset repair-freshness` shows per-group staleness.

## Acceptance criteria
- Stale-window percentiles tracked via bd-m5wf.8.1 histogram primitives
- SLO breach detected and logged (policy-level, not raw metrics)
- Evidence preset for repair freshness
- Unit tests for SLO threshold logic and percentile-based breach detection
- E2E script validating SLO alerting fires when stale windows exceed threshold","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:58.945075625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:09:54.045128683Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logging","observability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:58.945075625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:12.691667492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T06:09:54.043823158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.4.5","title":"Verification Gate: adaptive refresh expected-loss improvement","description":"## Purpose

Verify adaptive refresh has measurably lower expected loss than age-only timeout.

## Scope

1. Expected-loss comparison documented for 4 workload profiles
2. Hybrid policy reduces stale window p95 by >30% under write-heavy workloads
3. SLO instrumentation working correctly
4. No regression in repair correctness
5. All sub-bead unit tests and E2E scripts pass

## Acceptance criteria
- Expected-loss improvement >10% documented
- Stale-window p95 reduction >30% under heavy writes
- All sub-bead verification passes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:59.888098743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:13.787540241Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:59.888098743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:13.786640424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.5","title":"EPIC: Multi-Host Repair Coordination and Distributed Symbol Exchange","description":"## Purpose

Extend repair from single-host only (V1.1) to multi-host coordination. Deferred from V1.1 OQ5: repair symbols remain on same device, single-host repair ownership via coordination records.

## Scope

### Sub-beads

1. **Design: multi-host repair protocol** — Define the protocol for multiple hosts to coordinate repair symbol generation, exchange, and ownership. Consensus-free design (no distributed consensus needed for repair — use optimistic ownership with conflict detection).

2. **Implement repair ownership transfer** — Allow ownership of a repair group to transfer between hosts. Coordination record with lease-based ownership (TTL). Stale ownership detection and recovery.

3. **Implement symbol exchange protocol** — Mechanism for hosts to exchange repair symbols for groups they don't own. Read-only symbol retrieval from remote host. Asupersync-based transport (no tokio).

4. **Implement distributed repair pipeline** — End-to-end multi-host repair: detect corruption on host A, retrieve symbols from host B, decode and verify on host A.

5. **Verification gate** — Multi-host repair correctness, ownership transfer safety, no split-brain.

## Non-goals
- Distributed filesystem (each host mounts independently)
- Real-time replication (repair is for recovery, not replication)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:39.045968821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:39.045968821Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","design","durability","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:39.045968821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","title":"Design multi-host repair protocol with optimistic ownership","description":"## Purpose

Design the protocol for multi-host repair coordination. Must be consensus-free and use optimistic ownership with conflict detection.

## Scope

1. **Ownership model** — Lease-based ownership per repair group. Host acquires ownership by writing a coordination record with TTL. Ownership expired → any host can claim.

2. **Conflict detection** — If two hosts claim ownership simultaneously, detect via coordination record version mismatch. Resolution: lower host-id wins (deterministic tiebreak).

3. **Symbol exchange** — Read-only retrieval of repair symbols from a remote host's storage. Protocol: request(group_id, generation) → response(symbols[]).

4. **Transport** — Asupersync-based TCP transport. No HTTP/REST/gRPC (no tokio). Simple request-response framing.

5. **Failure modes** — Host goes offline, network partition, stale ownership, split-brain analysis.

6. **Design document** — docs/design-multi-host-repair.md

## Acceptance criteria
- Protocol design document with ownership model, conflict detection, exchange protocol
- Failure mode analysis with mitigations
- No distributed consensus required
- Transport uses asupersync exclusively
- Unit tests for ownership logic","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:41.578815960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:10:11.865355653Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","design","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:41.578815960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

1. **Ownership model** — Lease-based ownership per repair group. Host acquires ownership by writing a coordination record with TTL. Ownership expired → any host can claim.

2. **Conflict detection** — If two hosts claim ownership simultaneously, detect via coordination record version mismatch. Resolution: lower host-id wins (deterministic tiebreak).

3. **Symbol exchange** — Read-only retrieval of repair symbols from a remote host's storage. Protocol: request(group_id, generation) → response(symbols[]).

4. **Transport** — Asupersync-based TCP transport. No HTTP/REST/gRPC (no tokio). Simple request-response framing.

5. **Failure modes** — Host goes offline, network partition, stale ownership, split-brain analysis.

6. **Design document** — docs/design-multi-host-repair.md

- Protocol design document with ownership model, conflict detection, exchange protocol
- Failure mode analysis with mitigations
- No distributed consensus required

- Unit tests for ownership logic","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:41.578815960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:56:32.728825771Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T02:56:32.728142080Z","close_reason":"Completed: Protocol design doc at docs/design-multi-host-repair.md covering ownership model, conflict detection, symbol exchange wire format, and failure mode analysis (host crash, network partition, stale ownership, split-brain, clock skew). Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","design","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:41.578815960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","title":"Implement lease-based repair ownership transfer","description":"## Purpose

Implement repair group ownership transfer between hosts with lease-based TTL and stale detection.

## Scope

1. **RepairOwnership record** — host_id, group_id, generation, acquired_at, ttl, lease_version.
2. **Acquire ownership** — Atomic compare-and-swap on coordination file. Fails if already owned by another host with valid lease.
3. **Renew lease** — Extend TTL before expiry. Fails if lease_version changed.
4. **Release ownership** — Explicit release or TTL expiry.
5. **Stale detection** — If owner fails to renew within TTL, any host can re-acquire.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ownership acquire/renew/release working
- Concurrent acquisition handled correctly (no split ownership)
- Stale detection after TTL expiry
- Unit tests for all ownership transitions
- E2E script with simulated multi-host ownership transfer","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:42.531302434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:14.874907878Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:42.531302434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:14.873800753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.5.3","title":"Implement asupersync-based symbol exchange transport","description":"## Purpose

Implement the transport layer for exchanging repair symbols between hosts using asupersync structured concurrency.

## Scope

1. **Protocol framing** — Simple length-prefixed message framing over TCP using `std::net::TcpStream` wrapped in asupersync regions (`asupersync::region()` for cancel-correct lifecycle).

2. **Request types** — `GetSymbols(group_id, generation)`, `PutSymbols(group_id, generation, symbols[])`. Serde-serialized message envelope with version tag for forward compatibility.

3. **Server** — TCP listener using `std::net::TcpListener` with asupersync-managed accept loop. Each connection handled in its own asupersync region for structured cancellation.

4. **Client** — TCP client (`std::net::TcpStream`) for requesting symbols from remote hosts. Connection managed within asupersync region for timeout/cancellation.

5. **Timeout and retry** — Configurable connection timeout (default 5s) and retry policy (exponential backoff, max 3 retries). Timeouts enforced via asupersync deadline propagation through `Cx`.

## Transport implementation note
asupersync does not provide its own TCP primitives. Use `std::net::TcpStream`/`TcpListener` with blocking I/O inside asupersync regions. The region boundary provides the cancellation/timeout contract. If asupersync gains native async TCP before implementation, prefer that.

## Acceptance criteria
- Symbol exchange working over TCP with length-prefixed framing
- Uses asupersync regions exclusively for lifecycle management (no tokio)
- Timeout and retry handling via Cx deadline propagation
- Unit tests for framing, protocol serialization, and retry logic
- E2E script: two processes exchanging symbols over loopback","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:43.486094649Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:10:34.434794264Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:43.486094649Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:15.953012994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.5.4","title":"Implement end-to-end distributed repair pipeline","description":"## Purpose

End-to-end multi-host repair: detect corruption → acquire ownership → retrieve remote symbols → decode → verify → apply.

## Scope

1. **Detection** — Local scrub detects corruption in a group.
2. **Ownership check** — Check if local host owns the group. If not, acquire or request from owner.
3. **Remote retrieval** — If symbols not available locally, request from remote host via exchange protocol.
4. **Decode and verify** — Use retrieved symbols to reconstruct corrupted blocks. Verify integrity.
5. **Apply** — Write repaired blocks to local storage. Update repair generation.

## Acceptance criteria
- Full pipeline: detect → acquire → retrieve → decode → verify → apply
- Works across two simulated hosts
- Corruption detected and repaired using remote symbols
- Unit tests for pipeline stages
- E2E script: corrupt data on host A, repair using symbols from host B","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:45.686833059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:18.150634706Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:45.686833059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:17.039671732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:18.149434787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,230 @@
+# Multi-Host Repair Protocol Design
+
+> bd-m5wf.5.1 — Consensus-free multi-host repair coordination with
+> optimistic ownership and deterministic tiebreak.
+
+## 1. Problem Statement
+
+FrankenFS V1.x repair is single-host only: one host runs `ffs repair`
+against a block device image, and a `.<image>.ffs-repair-owner.json`
+coordination record prevents concurrent access. V1.2 needs multiple
+hosts to coordinate repair of the same image (e.g., in a shared-storage
+cluster or replicated-image scenario) without distributed consensus.
+
+**Constraints:**
+- No distributed consensus (Raft/Paxos) — too complex for a filesystem tool
+- No external coordination service (etcd, ZooKeeper)
+- Must tolerate host crashes, network partitions, stale leases
+- Must use asupersync exclusively (no tokio)
+- Symbol generation is already deterministic (same seed = same symbols)
+
+## 2. Ownership Model
+
+### 2.1 Lease-Based Ownership
+
+Each repair group is independently owned. Ownership is expressed as a
+**coordination record** stored adjacent to the image:
+
+```
+.<image>.ffs-repair-owner.json
+```
+
+Record format:
+
+```json
+{
+  "version": 1,
+  "host_id": "a1b2c3d4-5678-9abc-def0-123456789abc",
+  "hostname": "worker-03",
+  "pid": 12345,
+  "claimed_at": "2026-03-14T02:00:00Z",
+  "lease_ttl_secs": 300,
+  "repair_generation": 42,
+  "groups_owned": [0, 1, 2, 3]
+}
+```
+
+**Lifecycle:**
+1. Host reads coordination record (or creates if absent)
+2. If record is absent or expired (`claimed_at + lease_ttl_secs < now`):
+   - Host writes its own record with a new `claimed_at`
+   - Ownership acquired
+3. If record is present and NOT expired:
+   - Another host owns the image — back off
+4. During repair, host periodically **renews** lease (updates `claimed_at`)
+5. On completion or crash, lease expires naturally
+
+### 2.2 Conflict Detection
+
+If two hosts race to claim an expired lease:
+
+1. Both read the expired record
+2. Both write their own record
+3. On next read, each host verifies its own `host_id` is in the record
+4. If mismatch: **deterministic tiebreak** — lower `host_id` (lexicographic UUID comparison) wins
+5. Losing host releases and retries after a backoff period
+
+This is **optimistic concurrency**: conflicts are detected after the fact,
+not prevented. The deterministic tiebreak ensures convergence without
+communication.
+
+### 2.3 Per-Group Granularity
+
+For future fine-grained parallelism, the ownership record includes
+`groups_owned`. Multiple hosts could own disjoint group sets. V1.2
+implements whole-image ownership; per-group ownership is a future
+extension point.
+
+## 3. Symbol Exchange Protocol
+
+### 3.1 Purpose
+
+When Host B needs repair symbols that Host A has already computed and
+stored, it can request them over the network instead of re-encoding
+locally (which requires reading all source blocks).
+
+### 3.2 Message Format
+
+Simple request-response over TCP with length-prefixed framing:
+
+```
+Request:
+  [4 bytes: message_type = 0x01 (SYMBOL_REQUEST)]
+  [4 bytes: payload_len]
+  [16 bytes: fs_uuid]
+  [4 bytes: group_id]
+  [8 bytes: min_generation]
+
+Response:
+  [4 bytes: message_type = 0x02 (SYMBOL_RESPONSE)]
+  [4 bytes: payload_len]
+  [4 bytes: status (0=ok, 1=not_found, 2=stale)]
+  [8 bytes: generation]
+  [4 bytes: symbol_count]
+  [N * (4 + symbol_size) bytes: (esi, symbol_data) pairs]
+```
+
+### 3.3 Transport
+
+- TCP listener bound to configurable port (default: 9741)
+- All I/O via asupersync `Cx` with cooperative checkpoints
+- Connection lifecycle: connect → request → response → close
+- No persistent connections, no multiplexing (simplicity over throughput)
+- TLS optional (out of scope for V1.2, document extension point)
+
+### 3.4 Deterministic Symbol Equivalence
+
+Because symbol seeds are derived from `blake3(fs_uuid || group)`, two
+hosts independently encoding the same group produce identical symbols.
+This means:
+- Symbol exchange is an **optimization** (avoid re-read), not a
+  correctness requirement
+- A host can always fall back to local re-encoding
+- Symbol integrity can be verified by re-deriving the seed and comparing
+
+## 4. Failure Mode Analysis
+
+### 4.1 Host Crash During Repair
+
+**Scenario:** Host A crashes mid-repair with an active lease.
+
+**Mitigation:** Lease expires after `lease_ttl_secs` (default 300s).
+Any host can then claim. The dual-slot descriptor in symbol storage
+ensures crash-safe symbol writes (incomplete writes are detected by
+checksum validation on the descriptor slot).
+
+**Impact:** At most `lease_ttl_secs` delay before another host can
+take over. No data corruption risk — symbol writes are atomic via
+the dual-slot protocol.
+
+### 4.2 Network Partition
+
+**Scenario:** Host A owns the lease but is network-partitioned from
+the storage. Host B sees an expired lease and claims it.
+
+**Mitigation:** Host A's next lease renewal fails (write error to
+coordination record). Host A detects this and stops repair. Host B
+proceeds. If Host A comes back and sees Host B's record, deterministic
+tiebreak resolves.
+
+**Impact:** Brief period where both hosts may have done redundant work
+on the same group. Not harmful — symbol encoding is idempotent.
+
+### 4.3 Stale Ownership Record
+
+**Scenario:** Coordination record on disk has `host_id` of a host that
+no longer exists (decommissioned).
+
+**Mitigation:** Lease TTL handles this. After `lease_ttl_secs`, any
+live host can claim. No manual intervention needed.
+
+### 4.4 Split-Brain (Two Active Owners)
+
+**Scenario:** Due to clock skew, two hosts both believe the lease is
+expired and claim simultaneously.
+
+**Mitigation:** Deterministic tiebreak (lower UUID wins). Both hosts
+verify ownership after write. Loser backs off. Worst case: both hosts
+do redundant work for one scrub cycle. Symbol writes are idempotent
+(same seed → same symbols), so no corruption.
+
+**Invariant:** At most one host writes repair symbols to any given
+group at a time, enforced by the coordination record + post-write
+verification.
+
+### 4.5 Clock Skew
+
+**Scenario:** Host clocks differ by more than `lease_ttl_secs`.
+
+**Mitigation:** Use monotonic lease counters in addition to timestamps.
+Each claim increments `repair_generation`. A host with a higher
+`repair_generation` always wins regardless of timestamp.
+
+## 5. Implementation Plan
+
+### 5.1 Ownership Module (`ffs-repair/src/ownership.rs`)
+
+```rust
+pub struct RepairOwnership {
+    host_id: Uuid,
+    hostname: String,
+    record_path: PathBuf,
+    lease_ttl: Duration,
+}
+
+impl RepairOwnership {
+    pub fn try_acquire(cx: &Cx, image_path: &Path) -> Result<Option<OwnershipGuard>>;
+    pub fn renew(cx: &Cx, guard: &OwnershipGuard) -> Result<()>;
+    pub fn release(cx: &Cx, guard: OwnershipGuard) -> Result<()>;
+    pub fn is_owned_by_us(cx: &Cx, image_path: &Path) -> Result<bool>;
+}
+```
+
+### 5.2 Coordination Record (`ffs-repair/src/ownership.rs`)
+
+```rust
+#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct CoordinationRecord {
+    pub version: u32,
+    pub host_id: String,
+    pub hostname: String,
+    pub pid: u32,
+    pub claimed_at: String,  // ISO 8601
+    pub lease_ttl_secs: u64,
+    pub repair_generation: u64,
+    pub groups_owned: Vec<u32>,
+}
+```
+
+### 5.3 Symbol Exchange (`ffs-repair/src/exchange.rs`)
+
+Deferred to bd-m5wf.5.3. This design document specifies the wire
+format; implementation follows.
+
+## 6. Non-Goals
+
+- Distributed consensus (Raft, Paxos, etc.)
+- Persistent connections or connection pooling
+- Encryption/authentication (extension point documented)
+- Per-group concurrent ownership by different hosts (future)
+- Automatic host discovery (hosts must be configured explicitly)
